name: Proto-Dravidian (family node)
notes: STRUCTURAL ANCESTRY / SUBSTRATE NODE (not a modelled G2P target). Proto-Dravidian is the reconstructed common ancestor of the Dravidian family (South, Central, North Dravidian). It is referenced here only as the leading candidate substrate of the Indo-Aryan languages — the retroflex series (ʈ ɖ ɳ ɭ) that distinguishes Indo-Aryan from the rest of Indo-European is widely attributed to Dravidian (and Munda) substratal influence. No phonology is claimed for G2P; the inventory is a placeholder sketch (afa.json / trk.json stub pattern). Glottolog drav1251.

sources.author: Krishnamurti, Bh.
sources.title: The Dravidian Languages
sources.notes: Reference for Proto-Dravidian and the Dravidian substrate hypothesis for Indo-Aryan retroflexion (structural node only).

location.notes: Glottolog's computed centroid for the Dravidian family — a summary of a whole clade's range, not a place anyone speaks.
